title: senecio wairauensis
intro: Senecio wairauensis, also known as Wairau ragwort, is a rare and endangered plant species native to New Zealand. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a purgative and to reduce inflammation. It has been used in traditional Māori medicine for various health benefits, including treating digestive issues and skin conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use, although caution is advised due to potential toxicity.
